brand_system.category: AI meal planner + auto-ordering for delivery (health-tech / food-tech consumer app)
brand_system.audience: Metro IT / tech professionals, 26-35, in Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / Gurgaon who already order delivery 12-25x/month and pay for Swiggy One plus a health app ("Deadline Deepak"); fitness / macro-tracking users ("Macro Meera") next.
brand_system.positioning: Rasa plans your month of meals to your macros and budget, and orders them a tap at a time. Convenience gets you in the door; macro adherence and a hard spend cap are what make Rasa worth paying for.
brand_system.promise: A month of meals, planned to your macros and budget, ordered with one tap — always inside your calorie target and your monthly cap, never behind your back.
differentiators: Plans a full 30 days ahead, not one order at a time.
Hits estimated calorie and macro targets aggregated across the day.
Enforces a hard monthly budget cap with per-meal envelopes and coupon optimization.
Cross-platform by design (Swiggy now; Zomato, Instamart, tiffin next) — the neutral orchestration seam the platforms structurally cannot build.
A portable preference-and-macro graph that gets smarter every meal, so leaving means starting over.
voice_principles: The competent friend who cooks — never a diet coach yelling macros.
Health framed as everyday balance; wellness estimates only, no medical or disease claims (FSSAI-safe).
Never a convenience-fee framing; always health/wellness value.

copy_slots.hero_subhead: A month of meals, planned to your macros and budget, ordered with one tap.
proof_points: Plans 30 days ahead, so you stop choosing daily.
Every meal fits your protein and calorie targets — estimated for you.
Eat out daily and still end the month under the number you set.
One-tap confirm before every order — smart, never behind your back.
Allergens are hard-blocked before anything reaches your cart.
copy_slots.offer_text: Answer a few questions once. Rasa builds a 30-day plan to your macros and budget and quietly gets each meal to your door through Swiggy — you just tap yes.

channel_calibration.website_hero: Warm, calm, and confident. Lead with relief and balance, not features. Short lines; 'Sorted your lunch, tap to confirm' energy. Never a diet-coach or convenience-fee pitch.
channel_calibration.app_ui: Quiet, precise, unobtrusive. Terse action copy ('tap to confirm', 'stayed under cap', 'swap it'). Numbers align and read at a glance; every macro figure labeled 'estimated'.
channel_calibration.email: Pragmatic and caring, like a friend checking in. Recap the plan and the spend clearly; no guilt, no urgency-baiting. Honest 'estimated' framing on all nutrition figures.
channel_calibration.ads: Hook on the named pain ('No 4pm what-do-I-eat') then the balance-and-budget payoff. Health/wellness value, never a convenience surcharge; no medical claims.

display.name: General Sans / Satoshi (warm humanist sans, medium weight)

body.name: Inter (highly legible neutral sans for menus, macros and numbers at small sizes)

data.name: Tabular-figure sans or mono (aligned figures for calories, macros, prices and the spend ledger)

imagery_direction.photography: Real, warm, top-down and close-up food on cream or wood; natural light, honest portions, no glossy fast-food gloss or artificial garnish.
imagery_direction.illustration: Simple line-and-fill food and bowl motifs in the palette; friendly, geometric, never cartoonish.
imagery_direction.environments: Everyday Indian home and desk contexts — a meal arriving between meetings — not aspirational studio kitchens.

do: Place top-left in app and marketing; center on splash and the plan-approval hero.
Keep minimum clear space equal to the height of the 'R' on all sides.
Use the approved variations: full wordmark 'Rasa', the 'R' bowl monogram, or monochrome cream-on-green.
Add a scrim before placing the warm mark on any food photo.
dont: Never stretch or distort the mark.
Never place the warm mark on a busy food photo without a scrim.

composition_motifs: Soft rounded rectangles and full circles echoing plates, bowls and the 'tap' target; generous corner radii.
Matte, flat surfaces on warm cream backgrounds; subtle paper-grain at most.
A light repeating motif of bowls / spice dots as a quiet background texture, never over content.
anti_patterns: Clinical, diet-app coldness or medical/laboratory framing.
Gradients, gloss, or glossy fast-food-style food photography.
Background pattern or texture placed over content.

validation.note: Visual assets (photography, illustration, icons, patterns, logo files) are deferred — waves 4-7 of the brandmint pipeline require image-gen infra and have not been generated. Only the 7-output strategic brand-DNA core and config exist so far.
